title: rubus caesius subsp. vrabelianus
intro: Rubus caesius subsp. vrabelianus, also known as the blue-black raspberry, is a subspecies of the black raspberry plant native to Eastern Europe. The medicinal properties of Rubus caesius subsp. vrabelianus include its high antioxidant content, anti-inflammatory properties, and ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria. The health benefits of consuming Rubus caesius subsp. vrabelianus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Rubus caesius subsp. vrabelianus include fresh or frozen fruit, jams, and infusions that can be consumed as a tea or used topically for skin issues.
